mtrl.desc: [BD-JB-1250] New Exploit (bluray) for PlayStation 4 supporting upto 12.50 firmware from Gezine

description.img: The PlayStation 4 has new BluRay exploit that supports upto 12.50 Firmware. Thanks to the efforts of developerGezine,who has released the source code and helloworld.iso to complete the proof of work of this recent exploit for the PS4. Below you can see the readme for the project. Also some community reaction and comments about the new exploit for the PS4 from Abkarino & Modded Warefare dives into the exploit in one of his latest video attached​

mtrl.desc: {CFW Release} 4.92.2 Evilnat Custom Firmware (w/ Cobra v8.5) - Includes: CEX/PEX/D-PEX) + noBD/BT

description.img: After a successful Open Beta for 4.92.2, PlayStation 3 CFW developer@Evilnathas nowOFFICIALLYupdated hisEvilnat Custom Firmware'sfor the PS3 to version4.92.2,A number of new changes and options have been added in this release for your PS3 FAT and Early Slim models capable of installing a CFW PUP.Various patches to the CFW have been made as well as various new additions to theCobra Payload, Also theXAI Pluginwas not left out, as there are some new options one being a newNew Overclock menuhas been introduced to the firmware. Now we no longer need a dedicated PUP to change overclock settings, it can be done directly on the XMB. All the details and changes can be found in Evilnat's release note's found below and also in the link provided at the bottom.​

mtrl.desc: ProtoPwn (Protokernel Exploit by pcm720) - Now all PS2s are hackable via MemoryCard (MILESTONE)

description.img: Breaking Newsin the PS2 Scene,as a NEW MemoryCard Exploit for Protokernel PS2s (SCPH-10000, SCPH-15000, and DTL-H10000(S)) just got released!!!It took 25 years to reach this point, butNOW ALL PS2s including ALL special models(Arcade, Prototype, Debug, Consumer-model, "special/custom builds")can load Homebrew WITHOUT DISCS!​

This means both CEX and...​

mtrl.desc: PS3HEN w/ Linux & Overclocking Support? BadHTAB A hardware-software based hypervisor (lv1) exploit

description.img: UPDATE 4 - (May 2 / June 9):@esc0rtd3whas created a fork of the BadHTAB Exploit with some changes such as an "Automatic reboot if glitch crashes ps3" feature among other changes to make it easier for testing the new exploit.(June 9)- ReadMe Updated in link belowUPDATE 2 & 3:Now, not only Linux but also Overclocking on PS3HEN is now possible w/BadHTAB exploit&PS3HEN (beta builds)that are currently supporting the exploit. This is still considered an experimental exploit & features soUse at your Risk. (See update tabs/links) for additional details and information on these advancements for the PlayStation 3.Original Article: Could Linux Support onPS3HENbe a reality with new exploit? Yes, and also other features but itsnot going to be for everyone. There is a newProof of Conceptreleased for the PS3 that has been implemented byKafuu(@aomsin2526)usingGeohot'soldhtab glitchon PS3HEN. Developer@zecoxaofirst started posting about this idea and the concept in Jan. of 2023 here inthe forum'sand has shared various thoughts and progress, with the latest progress being Kafuu's implementation.BadHTABis ahardware-softwarebased hypervisor (lv1) exploit,The exploit has a very low success rate of (5-10%)as GameOS has a much smaller "glitch window". As the readme states this not meant "for daily driver, it is for user's withskill and patienceonly.".​

mtrl.desc: PlayStation Home Revived and Enhanced by Home Laboratory!

description.img: Home is back on the XMB in 2025! Download and Play just like it was originally!I am pleased to present our extensive work on reviving and enhancingPlayStation Home. Led by AgentDark447, our team has developed an advanced, open-source iteration of this server platform, significantly expanding its capabilities beyond the original. Below, I outline the project’s scope, technical advancements, and access instructions for those interested in exploring or contributing.​

mtrl.desc: PS3 Toolset (by bguerville) with 4.92 support has been released.

description.img: If you have a Custom Firmware compatible PlayStation 3 Model and are running 4.92 Official Firmware and need to Jailbreak for the first time or if you updated your previous CFW installation by mistake to 4.92 OFW. Then the PS3 Toolset will exploit your PS3 via the console's web browser and visitingps3toolset.com website(see tutorial tab), then once the process has completed you will be able to install a Custom Firmware (such as 4.92 Evilnat) once again or for the first time.Your PlayStation 3 model must be able to install a CFW, these would be all the FAT models PS3 and Early production Slim Models. The easiest and most effective ways to determine if your PS3 can install a cfw​

mtrl.desc: 4.92 Littlebalup's DUALBOOT firmware Released

description.img: Since the4.92 firmware updatethings have been flowing out from the community in response. We have seenPS3HEN (& hfw) releasedfor 4.92 and we have seenEvilnat updateand release his cfw collection on 4.92. Now developer@littlebalupdrops by the forumand release the latestDualBoot (DB) firmwareto support 4.92 firmware.This release for Custom Firmware user's is a very useful firmware, if you use PSN still and do not want to go online with a modded system and risk a potential ban. As it allows you to use an Official firmware that can re-install a CFW without having re-exploiting the console with the toolset or flash writer. See full details in @littlebalups release notes about the Dualboot firmware concept.​

mtrl.desc: 4.92 Evilnat Custom Firmware (w/ Cobra v8.5) Released - CEX,PEX,D-PEX,DEX builds

description.img: UPDATExai_plugin:New Overclocking Tools (change on the fly) / New ps2netemu improvements added.Following the4.92 firmware updatedeveloper's from around the community have been updating various projects. This time around PS3 CFW developer@Evilnatdrops by the forumand leave's us with a suite of ps3 custom firmware's that support 4.92 firmware. 4 Flavor's (CEX, PEX, D-PEX & DEX) are available for 4.92 Evilnat CFW, depending on your console/taste, all builds are equipped with theCobra v8.5 payloadto provide expanded feature set over Standard CFW's.If you are already on PS3 CFW, updating is a breeze, you just need to install the new CFW PUP file via the official USB Method and you will be on Evilnat's latest release. If you have updated to 4.92 OFW you will have to wait, as you will have re-exploit your ps3 console to allow for a Custom Firmware installation once again. The good news is that developer@bguervilleshould be rolling out a new update soon for the PS3 Toolset that support 4.92 firmware. The offline tool CFW Flash Writer may take longer for the community to update it, but we hope to see an update for that tool soon as well.​

mtrl.desc: PS3HEN 3.4.0 + HFW 4.92 - Officially released by esc0rtd3w of Team PS3Xploit

description.img: PS3 Developer@esc0rtd3whas released the latest update toPS3HENwith support to recent firmware updateof 4.92. Along with the PS3HEN update is the release ofHybrid Firmware (HFW), which is just an official firmware (4.92) with the "silk_webkit.sprx" file replace from 4.82 OFW (that has a vulnerability patched in 4.83 OFW, the last attempt Sony made to curb homebrew). HFW was created by@Joonieand has been apart of this 4.92 release of well.There have been a number of new changes in this update marked v3.4.0 as it does not just provide 4.92 support but various changes as seen in the changelog provided​

mtrl.desc: PS3 4.92 Official FIrmware has been released - PS3 CFW & PS3HEN DO NOT UPDATE to 4.92

description.img: As expectedthe PlayStation 3 has receivedfirmware update 4.92, if you are currently usingPS3HENorCFWyouSHOULD NOT updateyour PS3 Firmware to 4.92 at this time. Otherwise you will be temporary losing your jailbreak's and ability to run homebrew on the console, until developer's release new exploits/jailbreaks that support the new 4.92 firmware update. There isa processin which need to take place for things to catch up, as developer's@bguerville@esc0rtd3w@Evilnatand other's havebeen readyfor the update to launch to get to work quickly.We learned last week that@bguervillehas an update for the PS3 Toolset with new features being deployed as the developer explains in these two post >1<​

mtrl.desc: Update 4.91.3 > > > [PS3 CFW] Evilnat's 4.91.2 Custom Firmware's Collection Released

description.img: UPDATEJanuary 2025:CFW Evilnat 4.91.3[BETA 1] released>>Click Here<<December '24:Merry Christmas and Happy Holiday's to everyone, PS3 Custom firmware developer@Evilnathas dropped by the psx-place forum's on this Christmas day to present the final build(4.91.2)of the developer's ps3 Custom Firmware suite, after a series of Open Beta releases, v4.91.2 now officially drops the "BETA" tag and in its final form for 4.91.2. For discussion and full details about this PS3 firmware release and the various cfw types offered in this suite of releases, checkout the developer's release thread here in the forum's detailing all the various features and functions in Evilnat's latest releases notes and details for4.91 Evilnat's CFW (w/ Cobra v8.5).​

mtrl.desc: New PS1/PS2 Emulation Features for PS3 CFW user's + Evilnat's 4.91.2-BETA 12

description.img: UPDATE: January 2025:ps2gxemu (for CECH-C/E ps3 models) has been updated by@kozarovv+ netemu to gxemu config conversion tool by@Zarhas been released, see details at bottomNov. 2024: Recently we have had some great news with developments surroundingPS1 & PS2 Emulation on the PS3 for Custom Firmware user'sas the emulator's that reside in the PlayStation 3 Firmware have receivedsome modded tweaksthanks to@kozarovv&@mrjaredbeta, The files can be added manually to many firmware's or you can update to the latest release ofEvilnat's 4.91.2 CFW (BETA 12)that have these newly modded emulator's already installed and ready to go thanks to@Evilnat.The changes and feature's depend on the PS2/PS1 emulator being used and also on which model of the PS3 (with PS2). Below you will find a brief explanation of the emulator, mixed in with the readme details and some added screenshot. Some of the feature's need more testing to see how affective they may be such as the partial antiblur and EE Overclocking/Underclocking patches. View all the details and related links found below.​

mtrl.desc: CFW Flash Writer - Unofficial 4.91 update

description.img: Developer@aldostoolshas released an unofficial update for theCFW Flash Writerto supportHFW 4.91on the PS3. This can be a secondary candidate for allowing Custom Firmware installations whenbguerville's PS3Toolset (https://www.ps3toolset.com/bgtoolset/)isn't accessible or a self hosting offline option. However, ThePs3toolset is still the preferred and safest method when available for hacking your capable ps3 model so its ready for PS3 Custom Firmware installations.​

mtrl.desc: PPPwn - PlayStation 4 PPPoE RCE - (The First PS4 Kernel RCE - Supporting upto FW 11.00) by TheFlow

description.img: Scene Developer & HackerTheFlowwho has been a legend in various PlayStation Scene's, has done it yet again!! This time the developer has released "The first PlayStation 4 Kernel RCE, Supporting FWs upto 11.00".With the newly released (PoC) jailbreak the community will be able to update popular Homebrew Enabler's (Mira & GoldHEN) to support firmware 11.00 with this new jailbreak that TheFlow has disclosed (CVE-2006-4304) through the bounty program and has been patched in the latest PS4 firmware available​

mtrl.desc: Official PS3 Toolset Update (v1.2.007) Release (4.91 Support)

description.img: FollowingSony firmware update of 4.91for the PS3, developer's across the scene have been updating various exploits and tools. We have seen developer@Evilnatrelease his4.91 PS3 Custom Firmware'searlier in the week. Then we recently just had@esc0rtd3w&@JoonieprovidePS3HEN (& HFW) updated with 4.91 Support. Now we have developer@bguervillewith an update to hisPS3 Toolsetto support the exploiting of 4.91 OFW for custom firmware installation for capable models.​

mtrl.desc: Official PS3HEN v3.3.0 + HFW Released for 4.91 Firmware from Team PS3Xploit

description.img: TheOfficial PS3HENexploit has beenupdated to v3.3.0with some new changes from@esc0rtd3wand@JoonieofTeam PS3Xploitto support 4.91. Also, developer Joonie has brought back the webkit exploit from 4.82 to 4.91 with the official HFW release(HFW = OFW with old webkit injected). Its best to use the official HFW when using the official PS3HEN release.​

mtrl.desc: [Update Re-Release] PS3 Custom Firmware - 4.91 Evilnat Cobra (8.4) CFW Released

description.img: UPDATE -See 1st post for details, Re-Release a few bugs correctedPlayStation 3 CFW developer@Evilnathas just released his much anticipated4.91 Evilnat Custom Firmwarefollowingthe recent routine updatefrom Sony when they dropped firmware 4.91 last week. If you are on any Custom Firmware currently all you have to do is install this CFW PuP by the USB method. You will not need to wait for an update to bguerville's PS3 Toolset to exploit your system (as its already exploited if you are running CFW).If you had updated to 4.91 OFW or by mistake installed a HFW you will have to wait for the PS3 Toolset to be updated. Developerbguervillewas waiting for the first 4.91 CFW to drop before making his update and that has just occurred with this release, so it should not be to long before we see bguerville provide a 4.91 update to the toolset as the developerdescribed herein the psx-place forums..​

mtrl.desc: PS3 4.91 Firmware Released - Do Not Update!!!

description.img: UPDATE:its now safe to update to 4.91. PS3HEN (& HFW), CFW & PS3 Toolset have been updated to support 4.91.PS3 Firmware 4.91 is now LiveThe expected once a year firmware update has been executed by Sony, PS3 4.91 Firmware is now Live. While an expected update, could this bring other changes to the firmware? Likely not but its still early to conclude as this update is fresh as of writing .Do not update, various exploits / tools / essential homebrew will need to be updated to support the latest firmware update of 4.91. See below for a short FAQ on 4.91, as there is always some confusions around the update process. .​

mtrl.desc: Movian M7 (by deank) - "libav" swapped for "ffmpeg" & slight name change

description.img: Developer@deankwho has been continuing development of the (modded version) of the original media center that started on the PS3(now runs on Android, Amazon Fire Stick, MacOS ,Windown ,Linux & Raspberry pi). The media center originally was named "Showtime" on the PS3 but later renamed to "Movian" by the original developerAndreas Omanyears ago.Since that time, we have not seen the official project do much in recent years (currentlyhome siteis down as of writing), but developer deank has been keeping the project alive and evolving (see changelogsover at brewologyfor all archived changes). One of the biggest changes you will see within Movian-M7 compared to older version, is the change from the older (abandoned) "libav" video decoders/parsers to the current "ffmpeg" that was just replaced in the recent M7 update . This should improve compatibility and make for a more up-to date and versatile media player for all supported platforms including the PS3.Also, deank has made a quick stop in the forum's, then to see a reported issue and provided a fix (seen here) that is now present in v7.0.2​

mtrl.desc: [Update v1.06] Itemzflow (PS5) - Homebrew & Game Manager with PS5 game Support

description.img: Following the release ofetaHEN(AIO Homebrew Enabler for the PS5)last month from@lightingModz.Now, the release ofItemzFlowhas arrived for the PlayStation 5 from developer @ligthingmodz. Originally thisHomebrew & Game Managerwas released on the PS4, but the developer has made a special Christmas Release now bringing the homebrew app for the PS5 that is handy for managing and preserving your personal backups.(JUN 27, 2024) Update: v1.06has been released with new improvements and features.​

mtrl.desc: Alien Resurrection - (A cheat code to unlock boot disc feature for backups)

description.img: This is a very interesting discovery or rather disclosure from one of the developer's of the PlayStation 1 (PS1) gameAlien Resurrection(Martin Piper) who had reached out to YouTuberMVGwho has been given permission to disclose something that has always been there all this time in the game, but needed a cheat code to unlock the ability to run backups on a stock retail PS1, acting as a boot disc for the swap method. MVG details the process here in this video:​

mtrl.desc: [Update v1.8B] etaHEN - AIO Homebrew enabler for the PS5

description.img: The PlayStation 5 has an early Christmas gift this year as developerLighting Modshas releasedetaHEN - AIO Homebrew Enabler for the PlayStation 5 (PS5), Currently there is not much homebrew to launch for the PS5, but the developer hasported his PS4 HB Store to the PlayStation 5and thanks to theability to run PS4 fpkgs on the PS5, there is various PS4 homebrew that can run such as some of Lapy's homebrew games.Update (Aug. 12):New update has been released by @ligthingmods, now bringing etaHEN to v1.8B, See changelog tab below or guthub links for latest changes and features incorporated in this update and other updates since its release that has jump started the PS5 community. .​

mtrl.desc: Celebrating Nine Year's of PSX-Place.com

description.img: The psx-place.com website celebrates its 9th birthday on this day, originally opening in2014onOctober 14to the public. The site remains a pillar in the PlayStation Homebrew / Hacking community, thanks to the tremendous developer & user support that has blessed our forums. The past 9 years we have witnessed some amazing things in these forums, we have seen idea's and theories mature into full blown exploits and projects, we have seen developer's share and bounce idea to each other and even teamed up, which have spawned and improved many projects/aspects in the community. We have seen many developer's choose the psx-place forum to host their Release/Support threads for their projects. It has been a destination for developer's and user's to learn more about the community and even take part in it. Truly a place where people can come together for a common goal and that goal has been getting the most out of our beloved PlayStation devices.​

mtrl.desc: Athena - A performant JavaScript Runtime Environment for the PS2

description.img: AthenaEnv (JavaScript-Runtime) and the bright future of development on the PS2!​

mtrl.desc: FuHEN Contest Submissions - The Four of Us Are Dying / Beat2x / Zik-Zak Vita / Sonic SMS 3 Timelines

description.img: The FuHEN - PS Vita Contest is underway and we have already 4 submissions that have been approved for entry: We have seen 3 Game Ports submitted and also a new Original Game for the PS Vita. If you have not heard yet, there is a a PS Vita Contest that is currently on going and the submission period for projects is fromJuly 22nd, 2023 September 22nd, 2023You can learn more about the contest prizes, rules and the submissions process at the official website.We have an ambitious Original Game that has been in the making for awhile and aims to provide AAA graphics and effects. The gameplay is still early but the developer has big plans for the game called "The Four of us are Dying".byJosh Allen / noradninja.this game is a Silent Hill 2/Alan Wake style survival horror game.In the Game Port category we have seen 3 entries approved we have seen PS Vita legendary developer@Rinnegatamanteprovide three projects the first being"Beat2k" (A Dance Dance Revolution Clone)and also the developer has entered"Zik-Zak Vita"which is a great game that testes your reaction time getting through each level , Then@Rinnegatamantealso submitted "Eldritch" is a first person shooter inspired by games like roguelikes, immersive sims, and H. P. Lovecraft. Then tou round out the first wave of entries we have seen developer@MDashKenter"Sonic SMS 3 Timelines"which is port of a fan made Sonic game that takes Sonic on a new adventure.​

mtrl.desc: The Future of the PlayStation 2 Internal Hard Drive by Berion

description.img: The Future of the PlayStation 2 Internal Hard DriveWe have recently started 2023, and the so-called PlayStation 2 console scene is surprisingly still doing well, yes, even better than ever before and better than on not one newer platform! And one of the leading projects that anyone interested can mention without a long moment's hesitation isOpen PS2 Loader- the successor to HDLoader and USB Advance, a program used to run games from USB, i.Link (FireWire), MX4SIO (a reader for e.g. SDXC in the Memory Card slot), the local network and the internal hard drive, whose operation is still stuck in the darkness of the Middle Ages.Before I get into specifics, it's worth recalling what the current, extremely exotic logical disk structure that Sony designed looks like, and which is supported by every software written to date that supports theinternalhard drive. Without understanding the APA, you won't understand the idea of a hybrid drive. It is only later that I will discuss the concept of building a Frankenstein with something modern, familiar, while providing a full or residual layer of compatibility.​

mtrl.desc: (Update) Baryon Sweeper: Unbrick PSP 1000, 2000, 3000 /PSP Slim 04g, 07g, 09g/ PSP Street 11g model

description.img: Update (May - 2023):Now PSP models Slim 04g, 07g, 09g models, & the PSP Street 11g model are now supported >> See Details from@zecoxaoin the post via>>this link<<Original Article (Jan 2021): For more than ten years PSP-3000 owner's have been used to the fact that their device couldn't be restored (unbricked) at home. Many fear the brick as if it's the worst nightmare that could ever happen when experimenting with your device. Even worse in 2021, now because warranty for all PSPs are over, so bricked later (psp) models go either for parts or are doomed to dust in boxes and drawers they'd never return from... that is, if this thread didn't appear with the release ofBaryon Sweeper R1:^)​

mtrl.desc: PS3HEN v3.2.2 - New Additions/ Fixes & Backported Firmware's Support from Team PS3Xploit.

description.img: Update 4/25/2023: PS3HEN 3.2.2 has been released. Check the changelog for details.We have seen a lot of developer's back in the fold after the4.90 official firmware updatethat hit the PlayStation 3 several weeks ago. We seen a couple weeks ago developer's@Joonieand@esc0rtd3wof thePs3Xploit Teamupdate Ps3HEN to v3.2.0, now we have a new update with the release of v3.2.1..Ps3HENis a Homebrew Enabler that is an alternative exploit to custom firmware (cfw) for models that can not install a CFW. unlikecustom firmwareon the ps3, Ps3HEN has the ability to run on all ps3 model's, while many feature that are in CFW can be found in PS3HEN it does not completely replicate the CFW experience so if your PS3 has the ability to run a CFW that is the better route, but Ps3HEN is a very powerful and capable exploit. See a CFW vs Ps3HEN comparison>here<for a rundown of feature's.In the update v3.2.1, there has been a number of changes and added support to Ps3HEN.@Jooniehas released a backport ofHFW (hybrid firmware) for 4.83, this was done as Ps3HEN has provided support for 4.83 FW, plus 4.84 DEX firmware is also now supported. There has been a number of other supported firmware and various other changes in the payload and plugin to PS3HEN, Plus this fixes the wMM bug that was creating freezing issue in v3.2.0. See latest changelogs provided below.​

mtrl.desc: Huge Vita News from #35C3- @yifanlu & @DaveeFTW presents their Talk "Viva la Vita Vida"

description.img: Following up with theirannouncement that they will introduce their Achievementsin "Vita Hacking" at the#35C3-Congress, both Developers@yifanluand@DaveeFTWfrom@TeamMoleculetoday presented their Talk:"Viva la Vita Vida". And in this Thread, you will learn what they got achievedwith their Hacks but alsowhat this means for the Communityof "Vita Hacking"in future.And to "spoil" you a little bit, we recommend thatyou will read this Articlewhilesitting on your chairin caseyou would come offbecause ofyour roar with laughter.For an overview about the #35C3-Congress in General, or where you can find allLivestreamsfrom this Talk as well from other important ones, please clickhere.​

mtrl.desc: RPCS3 (PS3 Emulator) - November 2018 Progress Report

description.img: Christmas is overand theYear 2018 is coming to an end, but we havestill to Talk abouttheProgress Reports forRPCS3from November and December. While they released theirReport for Novembertoday, their hard work still shows thatwith every new month, their Emulator is showinghuge improvementsby gettingmore and more Games runningwithin the Emulator. Last month was no exception. In fact, theTeam behind this wonderful Project(which you can still support by the way)celebrates a new milestonefor November since it is the first time, that thepercentage of Video Games in theirCompatibility Listhave been risenabove >80% for Titles, which are both "Playable" and "Ingame". Just in case if you wonder what this means now, it implies that from3038 Game Titlesfor the PS3, you canload and play with exactly 2444of them withminoror evenno issuesat all. Isn't this impressive? Well, I don't know what you feel now but when I read thishigh amount of Gameswhich aresupported, I needed tosavor this slowly with my tongue. But way more needles to say is the fact that wichsuch a hard workthis Team is keeping up, thegamble has already paid offnot only for the Team behind this Project, but alsofor the whole communityand I think we can already say thatplaying PS3 Games on a PCisn't just a niche anymore.​

mtrl.desc: News from #35C3 - @m0rph3us1987 presents his Talk about "Exploiting PS4 Video Apps"

description.img: Following up with hisannouncement back on December 9th, Developer@m0rph3us1987today presented his Talk at the #35C3-Stage: "Exploiting PS4 Video Apps". Since his talk was held in German Language, you willfind every important detail translated in this Article, so everyone can follow what he was presented for each Chart.For an overview about the #35C3 Congress in General or where you can find all Livestreams from this Talk as well from other important ones, please clickhere.​

mtrl.desc: (Replays Added) The 35th Chaos Communication Congress (#35C3) - Everything you need to know!

description.img: Hacking and Exploiting Video Game Consolesby both Hardware and Softwarealways had a high demand by many people, not only when we talk about Consoles who had the name "PlayStation" inside. In fact, a lot of Hacks and Exploits can come across aboard when we compare all Releases from a whole year, like in 2018. But as usual, at the end of every year,many Hackers and Developersfrom all different branchescome togetherto the city of Leipzig, Germany,to present their achievementsin front of the wide audience worldwide. So as for this year, theChaos Computer Club (CCC)welcomes you to join and to participate at the35th Chaos Communication Congress- or35C3in short - which willheld from 27th December until 30th December 2018. And because there were always many Hacks and Exploits presented for various Consoles from the "PlayStation-Family" in the past, thisThread will give you a good overviewfor - as the Title already suggests -Everything you need to knowto keep track to the newest "PlayStation-related" Hacks and Exploits. ThisThread will also keep updatedif you will miss any important presentation for example, soyou will be able to watch any Stream or Presentation in repeat. So better keep this Thread here bookmarked in your Favourites.​

mtrl.desc: Meet "GameBook" - An Interesting Kickstarter-Campaign to make your own PS4 portable!

description.img: Home Consolessuch as the PS4 or Xbox One are made forplaying them"at Home"right? Well, I bet there are at least a few people who already asked themselves several times:"Why I can't play Red Dead Redemption 2 while sitting on the train?"Sadlythe answer was mostly that"You can't"before,due to several technical problems. Sony had some good ideas with their PlayStation Vita to play"AAA Game Titles"on the go but lacked of actually those"AAA Game Titles"and due to expensive accessories you needed to get most of the Console. And playing on the Nintendo Switch is not satisfying for everyone. So you had either thechoice to buyone of those very expensive"Gaming Notebooks"for several Thousand Dollars or you read a book while getting to work or to school by train. But the People behind the"GameBook"had bothered oneself about this problematic question by starting aKickstarter-Campaignto introduce their newest invention to people like you who asked the same question from time to time. And the coolest thing about this Campaign is,you won't need to disassemble your PS4bit by bit to create your own"Custom PS4 Notebook"only to get the PS4 Hardware fitting inside a Notebook Case. Just use your regular PS4 and you are already on the go. How convenient is that?​

mtrl.desc: #PlayStationClassic Released & Hacked --- Everything you need to know!

description.img: And there it is. After other Console Developers like Nintendo, SEGA or ATARI re-released their older but wonderful and classic Home Consoles in it's new and tiny chassis marketed with some catchy words like"Mini","Retro"or"Flashback",Sonydecided "to jump on the bandwagon" byreleasing their own Classic Consolein a miniature format - namely the"PlayStation Classic". Announced back in September 2018, the PlayStation Classic is adedicated version of the Original PlayStation(in short"PS1"or as you would know it as"PSX")providing 20 Classic Video Game Titles built-in. And while reviews from other video gaming journalists showed already thatsadly, the PlayStation Classic is a bad example how to re-release a"Retro Console"compared to it's competitors, this Thread will help you withEverything you need to knowabout theConsole itself, it'sbuilt-in Video Gamesand how fast several well-knownDevelopersfrom other Sony Consolesare already tinkeringwith it tofix several ProblemsSony was too lazy for and toprovide additional FeatureslikeEmulatorsand otherHomebrewetc.​

mtrl.desc: Could Sony be working on a PSVITA successor? [UPDATE: No they don't!]

description.img: UPDATE (29th Nov.): A quick clarification - No this won't be a PSVITA successor!Thanks to the"sharp eyes"from our community member@Naked_Snake1995, thisPatent Filing doesn't describe a Cartridge using for a PSVITA successor, where he explaines thatthis Cartridge is already in use for a total different Sony Product sold in South Korea only.You can find hisexplanationdown below while the Original News is kept for reference only. I (Roxanne) have to apologize personally for any missunderstanding!​

mtrl.desc: PS4 4.74 Kernel Exploit released by @CelesteBlue - including "NoBD" Support !!!

description.img: Folks, we have to talk. Today, well-known Developer@CelesteBluereleased anew Kernel Exploit for System Firmware 4.74. Now you will probably think:"Wait a minute, we already have aKernel Exploit for System Firmware 5.05. Why I would need one for an older System Firmware?"This is a legitimate question and if you have"exploited"your PS4 using System Firmware 5.05 already, then you won't need to do anything. But you don't have to forget that back in Time, when Sony released newer System Firmwares after Version >4.74, like Version 5.05 for example, they included a"banana skin"forPS4 owners with a broken Blu-ray Drivewhere their Consoles arerefused to get updated by giving them the famous "E-801809A8" Error Codein return. And sincethose users weren't able to update their PS4's to System Firmware 5.05, they were likely beingstuck on Version 4.74 without having any chance to exploit their PS4'sat all. But luckily, Developers like@CelesteBlue- together in Collaboration with@SpecterDevand@notzecoxao- didn't forget those users by giving them aKernel Exploit for System Firmware 4.74, so they can overcome those"security measures"made by Sony and by making their Consoles updatable again, sothey can enjoy the latest PS4 Kernel Exploitas well!​

mtrl.desc: RPCS3 (PS3 Emulator) - October 2018 Progress Report

description.img: Well, that was fast. After they announced their (delayed)"Progress Report for September", the great Team behindRPCS3didn't wanted to"leave you out in the rain"again. So here is the"October Progress Report"which shows again how much they are hard-working to make this Emulator better and better by making more PS3 Games compatible with their Emulator. And speaking about PS3 Games, if you watched the latest Progress Reports from the last few months closely, you will have probably recognized that withevery new Progress Report, more and more AAA Titles were getting more Support and a better Compatibility from Time to Time, even with supporting a 4K Output Resolution.This month isn't a exception and the Team is proud to announce that"Yakuza Kenzan" is fully playable now.Now you will think:"Wait a Minute - I never heard about "Yakuza Kenzan" ???That's right, this is one Game of the"Yakuza Series"which was released in Japan only as"Ryū ga Gotoku Kenzan"so it never saw a"Western Release". That's even cooler that such aAAA Game which wasn't available for most of the people are now playable for everyone, even with a higher Resolution as the original PS3 would be able to do so.How"Butt ugly"is this? :P​

mtrl.desc: RPCS3 (PS3 Emulator) - September 2018 Progress Report

description.img: Yes, you are right. We are already in the beginning of November and you have to read the monthly Progress Report from September? Don't worry. The great Team behindRPCS3didn't forgot to keep you updated. They were just a little bit too busy to release their newest Progress Report. But before you leave, this month's Progress Report is worth reading as well since in this month, they well go with you"deeper"in terms of aTechnical Presentationby showing you the"inner Workings"of theTexture Cacheand how it was getting improved from time to time, which is very interesting. And not to forget that there are alsovarious improvements in AAA Game Titlessuch asUncharted 3,The Last of Us,Resistance: Fall of Manand many more! Do I have to say more? :P​

mtrl.desc: Dead Space v1.3 (Vita Port) Released by gl33ntwine

description.img: The release of v1.3Dead Space (Vita)has been released by developer @gl33ntwine, in this update the developer has made several improvements and enchanments with analog contols, FPS limiter option, to fix the audio cut off issue's the developer hopes increasing the audio buffer to 1MB will do the trick, See Complete changelog provided in the details below.In order to play this game you will need as quoted from the readme "Legally obtain your copy ofDead Space Mobile for Xperia Playin a form of an.apk file.The version for regular Android devices, also known as the latest/Google Play version,will not work!Make sure that your game isv1.1.33(this is the last one supported on Xperia Play and provided via EA Store back in 2012)." so be sure to grab the right game if you do not already own it.​

mtrl.desc: HENlo - WebKit+Kernel exploit chain for all PS Vita firmwares by TheFl0w

description.img: Well known console developer and hackerAndy Nguyen@theflow0has released an old project of his for the PS Vita a "WebKit+Kernel exploit chain for all PS Vita firmwares" the exploit works, but the developer never made into a full jailbreak as there really has never been a need. Its just another solution to have around but the most useful part may just be the research for others at this moment. The hacker has made a few comments about the release as we have attached below the quotes and related links to the project.This release is more for the development and developer's who wish to learn more as we already have solutions for the PS Vita and PSTV but the developer does hope to see someone pick up the project and make a full jailbreak out of it. As the developer states it could be a great learning experience for some to finish the project.​

mtrl.desc: XtremeEliteBoot + (PLUS) - First (limited-preview) Release!

description.img: PlayStation 2 DeveloperHowling Wolf & Chelseaknown here in the psx-place forums as(@HWNJ)released afirst OPEN Test-buildof their long-term project"XtremeEliteBoot + (PLUS)",although for the time being it is intentionally limited in some aspects (until the release of the first stable/full release)! Consider this release as a Preview/Showcase and Playground (especially for developers interested in LUA) of the full project that is come later on,​

mtrl.desc: RetroArch (PS2) - v1.14.0 - Adds exFAT (USB) Support + Support for new PS2 Driver's

description.img: The officialRetroArchproject has been updated to v1.14.0 and with this update a few changes have been adopted for the PS2 port, Thanks to scene developer and RetroArch contributor@fjtrujy(Francisco J. Trujillo)improvements now have been made, recently the developer has been tinkering with the PS2 and not too long ago was trying toadd compatibility for OpenGL , as well as trying toadding SDL support(2)(3)for the PS2 , since the dev has taken his attention to PS2 port of RetroArch to provideexFAT (USB) support+ support for thenewly created PS2 driver'sthat makes it now much easier for dev's to use the PS2's IO drivers​

mtrl.desc: Psxitarch v3 Released (A Linux distribution for PS4 designed to be light & fast)

description.img: The developer's over at PSXITA.it have released version 3.0 of the popular Linux distribution for the PS4 known as Psxitarch v3. Psxitarch Linux is "designed to be light, fast and low on resources (it occupies less than 370mb of ram)" In this update there have been a number of changes under the hood with new functionality and features. All features and details can be seen in the release details provided below, but some features include integration of the ps4fancontrol, to help keep your PS4 running smoothly Other changes include the ability to "Ability to switch from Vulkan (ACO) to Vulkan (LLVM) on the fly."v3 like previous versions have some included applications, some of those include RetroArch (Linux) / Dolphin / mupen64plus / PPSSPP are just some of the included emulators, Other software /utilities includeWindows Manager: / Web Browser: / File Manager:/ Text Editor:/ Archive Manager:/ Network Connection Manager: / Bluetooth Manager / Image Viewer / Video/Music Player / Terminal Emulator/ Partition Editor / Audio Device Manager / PDF Viewer / Wine/. See full detyails and installation help in the release notes provided below and at the source link attached​

mtrl.desc: PS3 Dynarec support on PicoDrive (Sega 32x & Improved Sega CD) on the horizon from OsirisX

description.img: Good news if your a fan of retro emulation and have a jailbroken PS3, as developer@OsirisXhas made a bit of a breakthrough for emulation on the PS3. Following OsirisX other emulation release's / contributions like theunofficial PS4 port of RetroArchand the progress of theRetroArch Psl1ght PS3 portThe developer is now tackling taking on a new adventure on the PS3.One of the issues on the PS3, has always been the supporting of later generations(the 3d era+)of emulation. While we have seen PS1 / PSP / PS2 emulated on the PS3, they are using the unlocked system's firmware emulator's from Sony,. homebrew emulation has not achieved the same success, without support of a dynarec and the emulators re-written to support such a dynarec on the PS3, user's have been stuck side-scrolling in their Retro experiances. We seen an early example years ago when developer IngPeria released a PoC PS1 emulator (not Sony's firmware one) with dynarec support, but not many other developer's took on the task for any other emulators.​

mtrl.desc: RetroArch (PS4) Official Release is progressing, could we see an official PS4 debut soon?

description.img: Could we see an official RetroArch PS4 debut soon? perhaps some recent clues suggest there is some preparations starting in that direction. A few days ago, PlayStation 4 developerfrangarcjhas prepared a PR on the officialRetroArch (libretro) githubto begin the process for "an official PS4 Release". There is no ETA to report as there appears to still be various task needed to complete this process, however this is a great sign to see for anyone hoping to see the official PS4 Release of the Retro experience.The official release will be powered by theOpen-Source SDK, where as the currentunofficial RetroArch PS4 portdoes not use the Open So​

mtrl.desc: Apollo Save Tool v1.1.0 - View bucanero latest changes to the PS Vita port

description.img: Developer@bucanerohas a new update forApollo Save Tool (Vita), as some of you may or may not know the developer also has aPS3andPS4version of the homebrew utility available for those platforms as well. The utility is a great app for your save game files as you can resign and unlock and even download save's from other from the database. The tool originally debuted on the PS3 when developer@bucaneroforked the GUI ofArtemis-a Cheat Engine by Dnawrkshp for the PS3Since thatPS3 releaseand many updates we seen aPS4 versionemerge and also aPS Vita port.​

mtrl.desc: Flycast-Vita (Dreamcast Emulator) v1.1: Fast GDRom Load option + Latest downstreamed commits

description.img: PlayStation Vita developer@Rinnegatamantecontinues the magic in the homebrew scene. Over the last weeks we have already seen various release's likeDoom64EX/VitaDB Downloader/RigelEngine Vita /Zelda3 Vitaand today the developer stopped by the forum to release v1.1 update ofFlycast-Vita (Dreamcast Emulator),in this update the option for "Fast GDRom Load" has been added, this will provide faster loading times, however the v1.1 chanelog also warns that some games may cause more issues then benefits with this option enables. Also in the update this port was Downstreamed to the latest official Flycast commit, Take note that BoxArt Support is now disabled by default. This should help performance if you have alot of roms in your directory.Find the installation process and setup along with some tips in the official release thread >here< in the psx-place forums​

mtrl.desc: PlayStation Home v0.41 (GDC Demo 2007) playable on HEN & CFW from DeViL303

description.img: PlayStation 3 developer@DeViL303has been working on a new project of late, taking a break from some of the fantastic XMB work and various firmware hacks and tweaks. The developer has recently set his sights on PlayStation Home and bringing some of the functionality back while preserving some of the history of the project. DeViL303 has managed to get alot working in the GDC Demo 2007 of Home. There are various notes and details provided below along with a video demonstration of what is working. See full details in the release thread linked​

mtrl.desc: [UPDATE x5] What is Evilnat brewing up in his next PS3 CFW Release? 4.89.3 in development

description.img: PlayStation 3 developer@Evilnathas been researching and developing some new features for his popularEvilnat's Cobra CFWfor the PS3. Sony may have dropped official updates, but the development community has not stopped support and continues to pack new features and functionality directly into the firmware. With 4.89.3now confirmedin development by@EvilnatSome of these features are still in the PoC stage and some should be implemented in the next stable release (once fully tested) of the CFW. Currently the community developer's only have access to 4.84 DEX firmware, so the developer is trying to find (if it exist) 4.89 DEX (or a higher then 4.84 DEX) but the developer seems to be experimenting with "porting 4.84 DEX Kernel and required modules to 4.89 (like Rebug REX/D-REX) and is working as expected until now". However the developer warns at this time its still needs alot of testing but its encouraging to see some great development still going into PlayStation 3 CFW and to the Cobra Payload.​

mtrl.desc: "The next big thing[?]" for PS1&PS2-Hacking? A small dive into a bright future via the MC-port!

description.img: The future of PS1&PS2-Hacking has JUST begun!​

mtrl.desc: PS4 Xplorer v2.0 (by Lapy) - Massive Overhaul of this File Manager with v2.0

description.img: Homebrew developerLapywho is known for his various releases on the PS4 and PS Vita, ranging from Games likeDuck Hunt Remaketo useful Utility apps like PS4 Xplorer /Ps4 App Lockjust to name some of the developer's many projects. Has now turn his attention to a massive overhaul of that popular utility for the PS4, AsPS4 Xplorerhas been upgraded from v1.37 to v2.0 with lots of improvements for this long overdue update, This feature-rich File Manager does all the basic features of a file manager well with some added features like PKG Installation / FTP Support / Translation support for additional languages and much more.​

mtrl.desc: Latest Vita Homebrew by @Rinnegatamante includes Flycast (Dreamcast) / Nanosaur / Wordle / Tomb Rai.

description.img: PS Vita Developer@Rinnegatamantehas shared several releases in the psx-place forum's over the past week(s), One of the highlights was released in recent days has been a major update toFlycast Vita-(A Sega Dreamcast emulator)for the PS Vita and PlaStation TV (PSTV) . Updating from the initial release ofv0.1 from Jan. 2021tonow v1.0.2with assistance from developer@Bythosfor the latest update.Earlier in the month of August we seen Rinnegatamante with two other ports released here in the forums with:Nanosaur Vita (A Port of Nanosaur)&Wordle SDL(A clone of Wordle),which are both welcome additions to the Vita collection. Developer rinnegatamante has been busy with various other release's as well over the past few months. In case you missed some of the other recent releases from@Rinnegatamantelike theTomb Raider 1 & 2 Classic Collectionsport to the Vita, you can also checkout his...​

mtrl.desc: OFFICIAL bguerville's PS3 Toolset v1.2.004 Update Now Live - Supporting 4.89 + NEW Features

description.img: Notice OFFICIAL PS3 Toolset is not currently online, will be back soon.See Link for more info:https://www.psx-place.com/threads/4...irmware-capable-models-status-warnings.38553/Beware there are some unofficial versions out there in the wild, They are incomplete and have a high brick risk they should not be consider mirror. When official PS3 Toolset is back online we will update you here,.Following the firmware update of 4.89 the scene was awaiting an update tobguerville's PS3 Toolsetso that CFW capable console who may of updated to 4.89 (or jailbreaking for the first time) can run the exploit and once again and be able to install CFW from 4.89. Now for those experienced CFW user's they knew they should wait and NOT UPDATE when a new firmware update goes live as those user's simply just waited for Evilnat's 4.89 Cobra CFW to be released. .Now back to the official toolset update, behind the scene's prior to the 4.89 update the v1.2 update was near deployment. So instead of making various updates, one to support 4.89 and one for v1.2 new feature's it made sense for the project to have one testing session and release in a single update for 4.89 but that meant it a bit of delay for the deployment since proper testing needed to be conducted with v1.2 features. However in that time period a Russian Scene site had found someone else's re-implementation(they forget to mention that)and patched worked together a toolset that looked like the real thing but it was hardly bguerville's original toolset nor did it offered the safety/quality or feature set of the real release.There have been various changes in v1.2004 but the much anticipatedFile Manager&xRegistry Editorwill still be rolled out in a future update that will arrive soon after some theming issue's are sorted. To quote developer@bguervilleabout the new features in this update the developer states "there is a few new features to play with other than 4.89 support, like some memory patching options including HAN, memory dumping, extended system information details and syscon error logs. Afaik the issue that led to crashes on page load on certain setups should be fixed" Additional details can be seen in the tabs below .​

mtrl.desc: (UPDATE 2 ) Thefl0w discloses"Blu-ray Disc Java Sandbox Escape" vulnerabilities (PS3, PS4, PS5)

description.img: Update (See Tabs Below PS3 / PS4):Since the disclosure several developer's have went on the hunt and we have seen PoC releases and TEST payload pushed, We have confirmation the PoC is working on 9.00, other have been testing impact on 9.03/.04. See all the details from developer's likesleirsgoevy /@zecoxao/ psxdev(aka bigboss)in the PS4 Tab. Some preliminary discussions about the PS3 impact have been discussed in this thread from various developer's like@bguervilleand@zecoxaoOriginal Article:DeveloperTheFl0wwho is well known in the Homebrew and Hacking in the PlayStation Community, via the hackerone program the hacker has yet again disclosed details on vulnerabilities (5) "bd-jb Blu-ray Disc Java Sandbox Escape" is affecting PS3, PS4, PS5.Sony has patched these vulnerabilities in recent firmware updates for the PS5 / PS4 with firmware update's of 5.00 FW for the PS5 and 9.50 FW for the PS4. These methods are also likely to work on the PS3 according to thefl0w (likely on the latest firmware).thefl0w has detailed some advantages this concept provides over a webkit exploit, you can see that information and various details in the "additional info" tab below, along with some clarification due to over hyping some aspects of this exploit..:​

mtrl.desc: RetroArch PS4 (OrbisDev Port) official release on the horizon (PS4 support added to mainline Repo)

description.img: If you follow thepsx-place twitter feed, you may of seen aretweet from libretro's officialaccount yesterday, announcing theRetroArch PS4code being merged and adopted into the mainline RetroArch code repo, this mean soon we could see the official release of RetroArch PS4, as the official team has accepted theOrbisdev Portsubmission from scene developer's . For sometime there has been an unofficial port ofRetroArch PS4by developer@OsirisX,who (unrelated to ps4) also shared recently his progress on thepsl1ght port for the PS3 hereforums, to help bring the PS3 back into the mainline RetroArch repo. As the official team removed the old PS3 port in favor of the Open Source psl1ght SDK.The same thing was happening with the PS4, while we had a very functionalunofficial RetroArch PS4 portas seen here in the forums by the dev, it was never adopted in the official project's mainline repo, because the official team preferred aOrbisDev (OpenSource SDK)PS4 port. Thanks to various ps4 developer's like@fjtrujy/@OsirisX/ @frangar&@psxdev. We now have a build that is close to an official release created with the OpenOrbis SDK.​

mtrl.desc: PS3HEN v3.1.1 & HFW Officially updated with 4.89 Support - (The PS3 Exploit for nonCFW PS3 Models)

description.img: UPDATE: v3.1.1 released with HOTFIX see changelog below for v3.1.1Following thefirmware update of 4.89that Sony rolled out last week, the reunion of ps3 developer's was set for the PS3 homebrew / hacking community, First we seen well known Ps3 Homebrew / CFW developer@Evilnatdelivered his4.89 Evilnat (Cobra 8.3)a few days ago for those models that can install a PS3 Custom Firmware, but for those console that can not you can get many CFW features: like the ability to launch homebrew and plugins with the PS3HEN exploit. As@esc0rtd3wof thePS3Xploit Teamhas just released PS3HEN and fellow scene developer@littlebaluphas delivered the special firmware (HFW) that restore the exploits from firmware 4.82 that allows for the execution of PS3HEN.In this official release of PS3HEN v3.1.0(& HFW)for 4.89 firmware, there has been various changes to PS3HEN from developer@esc0rtd3wand various scene developer's like@bucanero,@sandungasand also@xps3rimentshave contributed to as detailed in the changelogs for this latest release of PS3HEN (Official).​

mtrl.desc: 4.89 Evilnat CFW w/ Cobra v8.3 (CEX) (+ noBD /noBT builds)

description.img: Following the success of4.88 Evilnat CFW. well known PS3 developer@Evilnatis at it again with a new Custom Firmware Release for 4.89 with the release ofPs3 CFW 4.89 Evilnat Cobra (8.3) (CEX) , following the official firmware update from Sony last week. This CEX based firmware comes packed with the Cobra 8.3 injected into the firmware(making this a Cobra CFW).There is tons of features packed into this firmware that coming from a top tier of developer's who has a strong reputation in the PS3 Homebrew / Hacking scene. The developer did not forget about the damage console with noBD / noBT builds of the Custom firmware also available Checkout all the details below.PS3HEN, BG Toolset and HFW Official releases are also coming soon from their respective creators , but be patient as important test are being conducted and BEWARE of unofficial releases (of PS3HEN/HFW) there has been some attempts emerging that may encounter issues from improper creations and untested attempts, best to wait for the creator's of these methods to be released with the latest version, Stay tuned for those incoming if you do not have a CFW enabled PS3 model then look out soon for PS3HEN and HFW. But if you have a CFW enabled PS3 and need to jailbreak you will still need to wait for bgtoolset to get an update for 4.89 support. Stay tuned as these release's drop in the coming days..​

mtrl.desc: PS4Load (by bucanero) - Load SELF files over Network

description.img: Developer@bucanerohas another release for the PS4 Community, following the recentPS4 Apollo Save Toolport there is another well known PS3 utility for homebrew development that is making it's way to the PS4. Many have known aboutPS3Load/PS3LoadXbuilt fromPSL1GHT.Now for the PS4 there is a new build using the Open Orbis SDK and now we have PS4Load.With this homebrew utility you can load SELF files over a network, which is a handy utility for developer's testing apps throughout the development process. Any tools making it easier for PS4 Homebrew Development is a step forward in the right direction. If you want to start coding your PS4 project be sure to checkout thePS4 Homebrew Development sub-forumhere at psx-place for additional links and resources for developing your next Homebrew Project.​

mtrl.desc: 4.89 PS3 Firmware is now live. - Does it affect a Jailbroken / Homebrew Enabled PS3?

description.img: Latest Status Update (May 20)​

mtrl.desc: uKaruga demake (new) + VitaRTCW (Return to Castle Wolfenstien) v0.5 update by Rinnegatamante

description.img: Following up on the recent update ofvitaFF4 v1.2, developer@Rinnegatamantehas also updated another existing project withv0.5 of VitaRTCW(Return to Castle Wolfenstein based on iortcw) which incorporates the latest vitaGL changes and graphical enchantments like fixing of fog rending also a new Aim Assist Feature for single player has been added in this update. Then the vita developer also introduced a new homebrew release with a very interesting demake with the release ofnKaruga.A vertical shoot 'em up game that is a"demake popular bullet hell game Ikaruga, released in 2002 for Dreamcast and 2003 for Gamecube".​

mtrl.desc: FF4-Vita v.1.2: Final Fantasy IV (Android) port for PS-Vita by Rinnegatamante, frangarcj & TheFloW

description.img: PS Vita developer'sRinnegatamante,frangarcj&TheFloWhave updated their Wrapper/Port ofFinal Fantasy 4 (IV)(Android)for your PS Vita or PlayStation TV(& Vita TV}to version 1.2 with a few new changes and enchantments by updating to the latest vitaShaRK & vitaGL commits, Also included in v1.2 an added option to swapOandXbutton functionality and to round out the update the added possibility to "force english dub usage". was added in 1.2If you are fan of Final Fantasy and have a Homebrew enabled PS Vita (or PS TV), then you must checkout the other android ports ofFinal Fantasy III&Final Fantasy V (5)from this same trio of talented developer's. Also, checkout some of other projects by these developer's found at VitaDB.viewRinnegatamante,frangarcj&TheFloWpages for more excellent brew by these developer's who continue to share some great projects to us all. You will find great projects like a wrapper ports ofGrand Theft Auto: Chinatown Wars&Grand Theft Auto: San Andreasand many other projects that are now powered by the community supported PS Vita Platform, lead by some great developer's that have brought some amazing projects to the platform.​

mtrl.desc: pPlay v3.8: A homebrew Video Player now with PS4 Support by Cpasjuste

description.img: Homebrew enabled PlayStation 4's (PS4) have seen a new homebrew release from a developer who has been killing it with some great releases of late(some of those includedpNESpSNES&pFBNEOto name a few of the recent PS4 releases). Now PS4 devCpasjustehas ported another project from the Switch with the release ofpPlay (a new video player for the PS4). Starting in v3.6 (now resides at v3.8 time of this writing) the developer provided PS4 support for the project (source codehere) and since then provided several quick updates (in v3.7 & v3.8) to improve the PS4 port of this handy application for your PS4 Console.The PlayStation 4 has another Video Player for the PS4 calledPS4 Playerfrom developer Lapy, which is a useful app but an application that has only seen maintenance updates for sometime and has limited codec support . This video player (pPlay) aims to provided a needed solution for the PS4. The PS3 had a great media player inMovainand hopefully pPlay can emerge and become a must have option for your homebrew enabled PS4 as well..​

mtrl.desc: RetroArch (Community Edition) Beta 4 - Unofficial PS3 Port by Crystal

description.img: RetroArch(CommunityEdition)has reachedBeta 4with a recent update provided by PlayStation 3 Homebrew developer@Crystal,. RetroArch (CE) "is a community effort to restore a functional build for the homebrew PS3 Community,". The official port for the PS3 from the libretro team been temporary shelved during a code transition. So, that is whereRetroArch (CE)has been filling the gap in providing a port that has fixed some longstanding issues on the PS3 with a greater focus on the console itself. The result becomes one of the most stable RetroArch builds on the PS3 with more libretro core's (emulators) then ever to add to the RetroArch (frontend) for your Homebrew enabled PS3 powered by either your favoriteCFWorPS3HEN.,There is alot more information on this unofficial port along with various links and details for instructions to setup many of the libretro cores being used. Checkout the officialthread herein the PSX-Place forumsand join the discussion for this unofficial PS3 Port.​

mtrl.desc: PS4 EMC (SouthBridge) CFW for PHAT Models CUH-1000 / CUH-1100 (Tutorial by zecoxao)

description.img: Update (Additional Tutorial):Software method for 5.05 only(5.05 hackable CUH-1000 and CUH-1100 ONLY!):We had some interesting developments on thePlayStation 5's 4.03 firmware just recently, Now developer@zecoxaohas dropped news for the PS4 in the psx-place forums today with a new tutorial that i must say interesting for the PlayStation 4 (PS4) community, asPS4 PHAT Models (CUH-1000 / 1100)now has theability for a CFW (in the southbridge emc that acts like a second syscon). Now, this is an advanced task and you can do some damage to your PS4 if you do not follow the instructions and even so there is always a risk when your messing with internal flash (EMC in this case) ,So this is aUSE AT YOUR OWN RISKrelease.You have been warned of the potentials.This works on firmware upto 9.03, also works on devkits / early testkits From@zecoxao"This only grants permanence over the southbridge and syscon, but does not grant permanence over samu it's just over southbridge and syscon you can think of it as a second syscon" So in laymen terms this is not like your traditional PSP/PS3 CFW, but rather a firmware for the EMC chip not the actual PS4's firmware (don't confuse the two).,The CFW (EMC) will allow for various things like " making the console beep, change temperature limits, increase or decrease fan speed etc"​

mtrl.desc: Icon Mask ( update v1.05) Change the appearance of your PS4 icons

description.img: Update v1.05 ReleasedOriginal article: DeveloperLapyhas a new utility ps4 homebrew he has released recently along with two quick updates to provide additional functionality with the release ofIcon Mask. Many of you know Lapy from his various homebrew creations on the Ps4 / PS Vita. We have seen from Lapy Games titles likeDuck Hunt remake/Wild Gunman RemakeBrain Splitter& various others you can see in theLapy Games Collection. the developer has released various utility applications likePS4-Xplorer/PS4 Tempature&PS4 App LockNow withIcon Maskthe developer has gave user's an easy way to customizes Icons on the PS4's main menu, giving a custom look to the UI with a variety of "mask" that can be used with more planned to be added in the future.. Below are some of the details of Icon Mask (which currently sits at v1.02 and supports 9.00 FW)​

mtrl.desc: pFBNEO (FBA) / pSNES (SNES9x) / pNES (Nestopia) Released for PS4 (9.00 FW) ports by Cpasjuste

description.img: You may know developerCpasjustework from the PS Vita with releases likepFBA/pSNES / pNES, well the developer recently released 3 emulator ports for the PS4.pFBNEO(a Final Burn Alpha emulator)/pSNES(SNES9X (Super Nintendo) Emulator/pNES(Nestopia (Nintendo NES) emulator).These emulators are compatible with the 9.00 jailbreak. View each below for the corresponding emulator details​

mtrl.desc: A small writeup + code for ROP userland execution for PS5 firmware 4.03

description.img: Following the recent9.00 PS4 Jailbreak. developer's:Znullptr&ChendoChapnow recently have released a small write-up and some code examples forROPuserlandexecutionon PS5firmware 4.03. Now this isnota jailbreak. what we have is a userland exploit via webkit, an entry point essentially. A way to attack / control the kernel will be the next obstacle and typically the more difficult task to achieve but this provides a sorta foot into the door for future exploration and what hopes to be a future kernel exploit to combine with this entry point.However it may prove to also be a great window to learn more about the PS5 for developer's and hacker's as it still new and they are still trying to figure out how Sony has tried to keep other's out. The developer's have stated the PS5 security is vastly improved over the PS4. It will be a process and take sometime but looks like there is a crack open in 4.03 for more exploration. Will it lead to the front-door being busted wide open in the future and a full fledged jailbreak? Only time will tell in the meantime checkout the interesting write up below and what the experts have to say on this write-up... ​

mtrl.desc: [Update 3- v1.0.0 Released] Apollo Save Tool (new PS4 version) Released by bucanero

description.img: Update 3 (Jan. 30 2022)-v1.00 ReleasedFrequent PS3/PS4 developer seen in the psx-place forums@bucanerohas now officially released a new homebrew for the PS4(graduating from somerecent Beta testbuilds), Bringing a PS4 version of his popular PS3 homebrew utility for handling and managing saves games now for the PS4 Jailbroken user's(9.00 Support currently not supported, but will bein next update), TheApollo Save Toolis a homebrew app that runs on a homebrew enabled PS4 console and as PS3 CFW / HEN user;s will tell you its been a very useful utility for Save Games Management over the past couple of years with lots of handy features and frequent updates that have brought alot of new functionality and features. .Recently, the developer decided to try and bring this concept and homebrew tp the PS4 and the developer has managed to do just that with this recent release for the PS4. The development journey can be seen in hisdevelopment threadthat tracked progress of the PS4 version from the developerfound herein the forum's( also, here is the development thread forthe PS3 versionand also therelease threadhere for the PS3 app for those interested with a PS3.).View all the details of the latest PS4 release from developer@bucanerofound below​

mtrl.desc: [Homebrew Revival PS3.1] Featuring "UFO Racer" by ThatOtherDev

description.img: Homebrew Revialis a new feature at psx-place.com where we explore some great projects of the past to educate new user's on some homebrew they may not of known about and also to perhaps sparks some new development in various projects we spotlight in this series, checkout another exclusive psx-place series withThe Power Supplythat has interviewed various developer's who have powered the community (will continue with a new season of interviews soon).First up, we have a great homebrew game that was developed for the Wii and the PS3 by developerThatOtherDev(aka ThatOtherPerson)calledUFO Racer, it's a game with a great foundation and has alot of potential to be a really great homebrew title, but the original developer has not made an update since 2012, However the source code is available, using an early version of PSL1GHT.. Can this be a project that see's a rival in 2022, 10 years after it was released? Checkout some of the source code and the tutorials this developer wrote for the PS3. Also, take note during the source code release of UFO Racer and many other games created by ThatOtherDev. The developer states the following about using the source code: "Make something. There is an appalling, lack of original games being made in the homebrew community and I implore you to take part in changing this." see full statement in tab below.​

mtrl.desc: [Update v1.2] SATURN-FPKG - Convert Saturn Games into PS4 fPKGs!

description.img: Following the recentPS4 9.00 Jailbreak, Ps4 developer@Jabuhas a new tool for 9.00 FW , you may remember this developer's previous release's for the PS4 with theps2-fpkgandpsx-fpkgtools that allowed for Ps2 / Ps1 games into a fpkg's to install on a homebrew enabled PS4. Following that same concept of injecting custom roms into an fpkg the developer now has a new tool that convertsSega Saturngames into PS4 fpkgs. With the release of "Cotton Guardian Force Saturn Tribute"(a Sega Saturn Game)for the PS4, it has equipped a useful Sega Saturn emulator that is said to be based off theSSF emulator. (According to ZiL0G80) and performing well. Upon seeing this game release is when developer@Jabuinterest peaked and then decided to develop and release the following tool to make running your Sega Saturn games easily on your homebrew equipped PS4 (9.00 FW).....​

mtrl.desc: TeamFAPS Release CEX2DEX for Vita

description.img: CEX2DEX for the PS VitaBy Team Fapps**PSTV & VitaTV planned for future date**​

mtrl.desc: PS4 Firmware 9.00 Jailbreak Update (Released)

description.img: "In this project you will find an implementation that tries to make use of a filesystem bug for the Playstation 4 on firmware 9.00. The bug was found while diffing the 9.00 and 9.03 kernels. It will require a drive with a modified exfat filesystem. Successfully triggering it will allow you to run arbitrary code as kernel, to allow jailbreaking and kernel-level modifications to the system. will launch the usual payload launcher (on port 9020).- via project's official readme"PS4 Firmware 9.00Jailbreak Released(awesome work bychendochap&@Znullptr)​

mtrl.desc: 4.88.2 Evilnat Cobra (8.3) Custom FirmWare (CFW-CEX) Released

description.img: UPDATE 4.88.2:PS3 Developer@Evilnathas updated and released4.88.2Evilnat (Cobra 8.3) CFW ,there is a number of new options like an added File manager, Option to toggle Cobra version, and various other new options detailed in the changlog provided below. Also the developer has provided a number of fixes and improvements, View all the details of 4.88.2 release of this latest CFW by@Evilnat​

mtrl.desc: RetroArch (PS2) Progress : New Cores incoming, gpSP (GameBoy Advance @ Full-Speed)

description.img: Emulation on a Homebrew Enabled PS2 Console has some great progress in the past year plus, thanks to still very much active PS2 development scene. Developer's like@fjtrujyhasported RetroArchto the PS2 about and since then has been adding more and more support for additional emulation with additional libretro core's., Recently the developer previewed some big things that have been in the pipeline for the PlayStation 2 port of RetroArch (a multi-system emulator).The PS2 now has a new full speedGBA (GameBoy Advance) emulatorcoming soon with thegpsplibretro corenow running very well @ full speed with the work of@fjtrujyand@davidgfnetcollaborating to bring dynarec to provide a full speed gba emu on the PS2 Console. Also, the Lutro Core (Lua) improvements are right around the corner as well with some progress being made (seen here) to the core . See some of the progress that has been made on the PS2 port of RetroArch so far in 2021 below.​

mtrl.desc: jfduke3d-vita + jfsw-vita + vitaRTCW released + Doom3 Port in the works

description.img: Following theKYUHEN Homebrew Contest, the PS Vita platform has been getting some new homebrew updates and ports from a familiar developer to all in the PS Vita community. Developer@Rinnegatamanterecently provided updates toVitaQuakeIII (v1.0),re3-gta (v1.4)andVitaVoyager (v0.8)Following those updates another project seen an update with the release ofVitaRTCW (v0.4)here in the psx-place forum's,.Now comes the introduction of a few new projects, some have been released already here in the psx-place.com forums,1st up we havefsw-vita - Port of Shadow Warrior Classicand then today we seen the release ofjfduke3d-vita iport of jfduke3d (JonoF's Duke 3D).​

mtrl.desc: KYUHEN PS Vita / PSTV Homebrew Contest Winner's have been announced!!

description.img: After a couple months of development of various homebrew projects for the PS Vita and PlayStation TV, theKYUHEN Contestfor the PS Vita has came to a close recently and now the Winners have been Announced (top 3 from each category). The Full Rankings (& Doiwnloads) can be found on the "Result" Page on the Contest's Official Website. The Contest yielded36 entries(about the same as GekiHEN Contest (39))that spawned across the following 4 categories:Game 1 , Game 2, Utility, Port. No doubt has been some impressive showing and some great projects that are now available on your Homebrew Enabled PS Vita or PlayStation TV(vita tv).​

mtrl.desc: re3-Vita (Grand Theft Auto III port) updated to v1.4

description.img: Recently the re3-vita (GTA III Port)is back with a new update with the release of version 1.4, this update provided by developer@Rinnegatamante, who has recently updatedVitaQuakeIII (v1.0)&VitaVoyager (v0.8)Has now updated this amazing vita port of re3 with various fixes for all "game-breaking issues affecting previous release's" and v1.4 also includes some features like the inclusion of a Game Manual. To round out the update we have support for video playback now in v1.4 and instructions in how to get your Video's converted to work on the PS Vita / PSTV, Checkout all the details below:​

mtrl.desc: VitaQuakeIII v1.0 + VitaVoyager v0.8: New updates to vitaGL provides new performance boost !

description.img: Developer@Rinnegatamantedropped by the forums here at psx-place.com to release a couple of new items for the homebrew community. First up we have an update toVitaQuakeIIIwith the release of version 1.0. This update has provided the port withMultitexturing supportandTexture CombinerWhat this does, is improves performance on the PS Vita and provides support for features like dynamic light that previously has been disabled prior to this update (see video below for a showcase of examples). In regards to the recentadvancements to VitaGLthe developer states "Expect a branch of releases for idTech3 derivates plus maybe some new idTech 3 derivates ports in the future" be sure to keep a look out in the future here in thepsx-place forumsand @VitaDBfor when those release appear directly fromRinnegatamante.​

mtrl.desc: KYUHEN - A New Vita Homebrew Contest (Now Live - Submissions ends on May 27, 2021)

description.img: There is a newVita Homebrew Contestthat is starting in the Vita Community, in the past we have seen theRejuvenate Contestthat helped spark some early development in the Vita Scene, then later on we had theGekiHEN Contestwhich psx-place took a part in along with other scene sites, now we have the a new contest for the Vita Community calledKYUHEN,Spearheaded byCimmerian , 2old4Gaming , frangarcjwith assistance from psx-place.com / wololo.net / playstationhax.xyz / Customprotocol.com .The Contest will consist of a2 month development periodand spanacross 4 categories. Those categories include 2 separate"Games"categories one for SDK Homebrew and one for Project powered by ported engine's such as Unity or GameMaker. Then there will be a separate category for"Ports"of games/emulators ect.. Then to round out the last category there is"Utilities"and this one is open to both applications and plugins .​

mtrl.desc: [Update] Apollo Save Tool v1.8.4 released

description.img: Update v1.8.4 ReleasedOriginal Introduction:PS3 development has been seeing an upkick lately and developer@bucanerohas been one of the developers playing a part. The developer has recently updatedArtemis with new supportand also releasedPKGi (PS3)but now the developer shares a great utility that involves PS3 Game Saves calledApollo, built utilizing the Artemis GUI this homebrew capable of running on CFW and PS3HEN will allow you to Patch & Resign your Game Saves / Download Game Saves / Unlock Game Saves all from the convenience of your PS3 console and not PC tools are required. Below you can see the release thread from the developer himself: So i'll let@bucanerotake it from here with another great release:​

mtrl.desc: (Update 1.1) Grand Theft Auto: Chinatown Wars (GTA CTW) Vita Port by @theflow0 / @Rinnegatamante

description.img: Update v1.1 ReleasedOriginal Article (March 17):The duo of PS Vita legends developer'sTheFlowand@Rinnegatamanteteam up again with another great release for the PS Vita Community. We have seen the family ofGrand Theft Auto Seriesmake the journey to the PS Vita with various ports and techniques but have we been missing one from the PSP era in the form of a native port? First we seen the release ofre3 (GTA III)followed byreVC (GTA: Vice City)which utilized a reversed engineered engine .. Then several weeks ago we seen those two classic GTA game's followed by what may be the most iconic game in the series withgtaSA_Vita (GTA: San Andreas)as we just witnessed updatev1.6released Whichtook a different approach and utilized the Android port of the game and puts TheFlow's kubridge plugin to work serving as a wrapper for aiding in the porting some Android games to the PS Vita (seeMax Payneas another Android game ported)Now, that brings us to today's release with is a port of another GTA Classic from the PSP with the release ofGrand Theft Auto: Chinatown Wars, (GTA:CFW Vita) a 3D top view game from the series , LikeMax PayneandGTASAthis is a port of the Android version with magic from@Rinnegatamanteand TheFlow.​

mtrl.desc: {UPDATE] PS4 Jailbreak 7.50 /7.55 w/ Mira Support Released

description.img: UPDATE7.55 w/ Mira Support ReleasedOriginal Article (7.50) Not only did we just see aPS1 exploit released, the exploited PS4 firmware latter has extended a step to now firmware 7.50 has been officially exploited and released to the public as developerSleirsgoevyhas released the latest PS4 Jailbreak that has utilized the latest findings fromTheFlowin his disclosed bug that has the potential to work upto 7.55 but is currently targeted for 7.50 for this release.TheFlowhas tweeted several hints regarding 7.55 (tweet1/2) but 7.55 is yet to be solved by other,@zecoxaoprovided some details and shared via twitter see below.This is not a fully functional jailbreak yet as there is elements that are needed to be ported like the Mira/HEN and other Payload, so you should not update to this exploit from previous exploits yet. Let it mature, but if your on a PS4 on 7.50 or lower and missed out on previous exploits, Stay Tuned as this progresses,​

mtrl.desc: [PS3 XMB MOD] Now have Individual Gameboots for each emulator / system / homebrew launched

description.img: If you have an exploited PS3 and have been following some of the progress that@DeViL303has made with XMB modding ,then you already know about some of these great project's the xmb mod developer has been working on lately. For those of you new to his work, some recent mods from DeViL303 includeUltimate ToolboxandPixel Art possibilitiesand many more from the past . Now the developer has taken on enhancing the Gameboot animation when launching content from the PS3's XMB. Having the same boring sequence for all your content simply won't cut it anymore. Now withwebMAN MODhelping fuel the concept we now have the abilityto set individual Gameboots per contentsuch as bringing back those iconic start-ups from the PS1 and PS2 or having the system startups for your favorite retro roms. Well now you have that ability and more with the latest release from@DeViL303and the latest update to webMAN MOD from@aldostoolsthat helps power the concept the two have been working on the past several weeks seen here in the psx-place forums (here)​

mtrl.desc: tonyhax - A Software backup loader exploit for the PS1 (leverages save game exploit)

description.img: Looks like those Classic PlayStation Consoles are still making waves in the homebrew scene. We have seen vibrant development on the PS2 with just recent things likeOpenTunaand also recentMechaCon Firmware dumpedwhich can potentially bring various possibilities discussedhere. However the PS2 is not alone as its daddy the original OG the PlayStation 1 now has a Software Backup Loader Exploit that leverages saves game exploits from Tony Hawk Pro Skater 2 & 3 from developersocram8888. The developer has titled the project "tonyhax" and will work with both the PAL and NTSC versions of the gameThe developer has provided a technical write up and easy to follow explanation as well as a YouTube video we have also attached below. Checkout the latest exploit to hit the PS1.​

mtrl.desc: IRISMAN v4.87.1: Adds support for mounting Compressed ISO's ( ZIP, RAR or 7-Zip)

description.img: Following some of the recent advancements made towebMAN MOD, PlayStation 3 Homebrew developer@aldostoolshas now looked to incorporate a new feature in the PS3 Homebrew(CFW/PS3HEN) fbackup manager known asIRISMAN. It's currently a new and experimental feature added in the recent release ofv4.87.1that provides the backup manager the ability tomount compressed ISO in .zip / .7z / .rar formats. This features supports thePS3's Internal HDD or a FAT32 external storage device for PS1 / PSP / PS2 / PS3/ DVD / BluRay ISO's..​

mtrl.desc: Another new/old Exploit for the PS2! OpenTuna!

description.img: The year is 2021 and the PlayStation 2 Hacking Scene is still alive and kicking very well and we have a new/old Exploit for our PS2 to share!Even with later generations of PlayStation Console like the PS3 / PS4 / PS5 / PS Vita around, the PS2 is still thriving in the development / homebrew community, while serving a great audience for the console. We have seen the progression of several major projects and exploits in the past year for the PlayStation 2 (PS2) like the updates toOpen PS2 Loader (OPL)to version 1.0.0 andFreeDVDBootexploit.We now have"OpenTuna", brought to you by@alexparrado!OpenTunaIt is an open source version of another recent exploit found and developed back in late 2019 for the PS2 called theFortuna exploit.
